_comment: Run scaffolding deck for the exploratory product-journey pilot.

scenes.title: Product Journey Eval
scenes.subtitle: Skeptical user path across site/docs/onboarding + real project bug corpus
scenes.narration: Pilot implementation landed in a worktree under .worktrees so the branch stays isolated and reviewable.

scenes.title: Pilot state
scenes.narration: The first milestone is runnable from local checkout: one entrypoint, explicit project catalog, and real local corpora for the planned lanes.

cards.label: Corpus
cards.sub: gears-rust (4 armable fixtures) via existing external grader

cards.label: Local checks
cards.sub: status/check commands are deterministic and no-LLM by default

cards.label: Perspectives
cards.sub: postgresql and kubernetes cloned under ~/code and tracked as planned follow-up lanes

scenes.body: `python3 tools/product-journey/run.py --project gears-rust --mode check` succeeds for runner-mode targets and reports manifest-backed run command, while PostgreSQL and Kubernetes return planned-status checks for next-in-the-stack evidence capture.
scenes.narration: The check surface is the minimum viable control for orchestrator automation: deterministic readiness before any model-driven runs.

scenes.title: Latest check state

cells: set GEARS_RUST_REPO and run: GEARS_RUST_REPO=<path> make gears-bakeoff
local checkout exists under ~/code; define manifest + deterministic scoring/oracle contract
local checkout exists under ~/code; define size/CI constraints + manifest + perspective test harness

scenes.caption: Perspective checks stay explicit until each project gets a manifest/contract.

scenes.title: Local execution artifacts
scenes.caption: Deck is source-of-truth JSON in docs; rendered HTML stays in .artifacts/.

scenes.body: With this baseline stable, run `make web` (or a local `go run ./cmd/kitsoki web`) for A/B experiments, then switch `--project` to PostgreSQL and Kubernetes as those perspectives move to runner-ready with manifest-backed check/grade contracts.
scenes.narration: Evidence-first approach: every check emits to `.context` and every pilot step is represented in the deck.

scenes.body: Ran the harness path locally: `python3 tools/product-journey/run.py` plus `--project gears-rust --mode check` with `GEARS_RUST_REPO` set, and the runner now validates gears-rust through a no-local temp clone. PostgreSQL and Kubernetes are now cloned under `~/code`, so the runner and `tools/story-qa/run.py` can report those real local checkouts instead of abstract placeholders; they still remain planned until a corpus/manifest contract exists. The story-qa wrapper writes a transient report under `.context/`.
scenes.narration: The deck stays reviewable even when some project corpora are not yet instrumented; those gaps are surfaced as explicit plan items instead of silent successes.
